questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt:
With reference to the 'Agni Prime' missile, consider the following statements:
1. It is a two-stage, solid-fueled ballistic missile.
2. It is a canister-launched missile capable of carrying both conventional and nuclear warheads.
3. It utilizes a rail-based mobile launcher system.

How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Agni Prime is a two-stage, solid-fueled ballistic missile. Statement 2 is correct: It is a canister-launched missile capable of carrying both conventional and nuclear warheads. Statement 3 is correct: It was successfully launched from a rail-based mobile launcher system.
questions.takeaway: Agni Prime is a new-generation, solid-fueled, canisterized ballistic missile with dual payload capability (conventional and nuclear) and mobile launch options.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Agni Prime: DRDO & Strategic Force Command (SFC) successfully launched the Agni-Prime Missile from a Rail based Mobile launcher system... Two-stage, solid-fueled, canister-launched ballistic missile. It can carry both conventional and nuclear warheads.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt:
Consider the following missiles:
1. Akash
2. Nag
3. Trishul
4. Agni Prime

How many of the above missiles were developed under the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP)?
questions.explanation: Akash, Nag, and Trishul were developed under the IGMDP (started in 1983). Agni Prime is the first of the new generation of missiles developed after the end of the IGMDP, making it the only exception in the list.
questions.takeaway: The IGMDP included Prithvi, Trishul, Akash, and Nag. Agni Prime is a post-IGMDP development.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The missiles developed under the program were: Prithvi... Trishul... Akash... Nag... Agni Prime: ... First of the new generation of missiles after the end of the IGMDP.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt:
Consider the following pairs regarding the missiles developed under the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP):

Missile : Operational Type
1. Prithvi : Short-range surface-to-surface missile
2. Trishul : Medium-range surface-to-surface missile
3. Akash : Medium-range surface-to-air missile

How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched: Prithvi is a short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile. Pair 2 is incorrectly matched: Trishul is a short-range low-level surface-to-air missile, not a medium-range surface-to-surface missile. Pair 3 is correctly matched: Akash is a medium-range surface-to-air missile.
questions.takeaway: Under IGMDP, Prithvi is a surface-to-surface missile, while Trishul (short-range) and Akash (medium-range) are surface-to-air missiles.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Prithvi: Short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile. Trishul: Short-range low-level surface-to-air missile. Akash: Medium-range surface-to-air missile.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding the Agni Prime missile:
1. It was successfully launched by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) in collaboration with the Strategic Forces Command (SFC).
2. It is the first of the new generation of missiles developed after the end of the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP).

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The Agni Prime missile was successfully launched by DRDO and the Strategic Forces Command (SFC). It marks the first of the new generation of missiles developed after the conclusion of the IGMDP.
questions.takeaway: Agni Prime represents a post-IGMDP advancement in India's missile technology, tested jointly by DRDO and SFC.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Agni Prime: DRDO & Strategic Force Command (SFC) successfully launched the Agni-Prime Missile... First of the new generation of missiles after the end of the IGMDP.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: Which one of the following best describes the 'Nag' missile developed by India?

options.text: A medium-range surface-to-air missile designed to intercept low-flying aircraft.
A third-generation anti-tank missile developed under the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme.
A canister-launched ballistic missile capable of carrying nuclear warheads.

questions.explanation: Nag is a third-generation anti-tank missile developed as part of the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP). Option A describes Akash/Trishul, Option B describes Prithvi, and Option D describes Agni Prime.
questions.takeaway: The IGMDP included various specialized missiles, with Nag specifically designed for anti-tank warfare.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding India's missile development:
1. The Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP) was initiated in 1983 to attain self-sufficiency in missile technology.
2. Under the IGMDP, Prithvi was developed to intercept aerial targets, while Akash was developed for surface-to-surface strikes.
3. Trishul is a short-range low-level surface-to-air missile developed under the IGMDP.

How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The IGMDP started in 1983 for self-sufficiency in missile technology. Statement 2 is incorrect: Prithvi is a surface-to-surface missile, while Akash is a surface-to-air missile. Statement 3 is correct: Trishul is a short-range low-level surface-to-air missile.
questions.takeaway: The IGMDP developed a diverse range of missiles, including surface-to-surface (Prithvi) and surface-to-air (Trishul, Akash) systems.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: About IGMDP: Started in 1983 to attain self-sufficiency in the field of missile technology... Prithvi: Short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile. Trishul: Short-range low-level surface-to-air missile. Akash: Medium-range surface-to-air missile.
